_metadata.description: Controlled vocabulary for the 5 clinical_risk_enum tiers (critical, high, moderate, dose_dependent, low) in banned_recalled_ingredients.json. UI-bound vocab — carries full DISPLAY CONTRACT.
_metadata.purpose: Single source of truth for clinical-risk presentation in banned/recalled alerts. Each entry carries the locked display contract.
_metadata.alignment: Pipeline source: scripts/data/banned_recalled_ingredients.json `clinical_risk_enum` field (146 entries; live: critical=98, high=33, moderate=13, dose_dependent=1, low=1).

field_contract.name: Full user-facing label.
field_contract.severity_weight: Numeric weight (1=lowest, 5=highest) used in scoring tie-breaks.

clinical_risks.name: Critical risk
clinical_risks.notes: Documented serious adverse events including organ failure, hospitalization, or death. Most common tier in banned-recalled data.

clinical_risks.name: High risk
clinical_risks.notes: Significant safety concerns documented in clinical literature; serious adverse events reported but less consistently than critical tier.

clinical_risks.name: Moderate risk
clinical_risks.notes: Notable safety concerns at therapeutic doses. Use only with provider awareness and monitoring.

clinical_risks.name: Dose-dependent risk
clinical_risks.notes: Substance is safe at low doses but risk escalates with dose. Stay within published safe upper limits.

clinical_risks.name: Low risk
clinical_risks.notes: Minor or rare safety concerns; risk is generally low at typical doses but worth being aware of.
